trueOrFalse.story: Can you go to the park?
trueOrFalse.rule: You need BOTH to say YES to go!
trueOrFalse.outcomeTrue: 🎉 You can go to the park!

trueOrFalse.story: Can you go to the park?
trueOrFalse.rule: You need BOTH to say YES to go!
trueOrFalse.outcomeTrue: 🎉 You can go to the park!

trueOrFalse.story: Can you go to the park?
trueOrFalse.rule: You need BOTH to say YES to go!
trueOrFalse.outcomeTrue: 🎉 You can go to the park!

trueOrFalse.story: Can you go to the park?
trueOrFalse.rule: You need BOTH to say YES to go!
trueOrFalse.outcomeTrue: 🎉 You can go to the park!

trueOrFalse.rule: If you finished homework OR cleaned your room, you get ice cream!
If you finished homework OR cleaned your room, you get ice cream!
If you finished homework OR cleaned your room, you get ice cream!
If you finished homework OR cleaned your room, you get ice cream!
NOT flips it! If the light is ON, NOT makes it OFF!
NOT flips it! If the light is OFF, NOT makes it ON!
The robot needs BOTH batteries to be full to move!
The robot needs BOTH batteries to be full to move!
You can watch TV if you ate your veggies OR had a nap!
You can watch TV if you ate your veggies OR had a nap!
NOT flips it! If it is NOT sunny, then it must be raining!
NOT flips it! If it IS sunny, then it is NOT raining!
The rocket needs BOTH the fuel tank full AND the weather clear!
The rocket can launch if the fuel is ready OR the backup engine is on!

wordProblems.story: Tom has 5 apples. He eats 2. How many apples does he have left?
Sara has 4 red balloons and 3 blue balloons. How many balloons altogether?
wordProblems.hint: Add the red ones and the blue ones together.

wordProblems.story: There are 10 cookies on a plate. The kids eat 6. How many are left?
A frog jumps 3 times. Each jump is 4 steps. How many steps in total?
wordProblems.hint: How many groups of 4 do you have?

wordProblems.story: Mia has 8 stickers. She gives 3 to her friend. How many does she have now?
There are 6 birds in a tree. 4 more birds land. How many birds are there now?
wordProblems.hint: Add the birds that were there and the new ones.

wordProblems.story: Leo has 15 marbles. He loses 7. How many marbles does he have left?
There are 3 bags with 5 oranges in each bag. How many oranges are there?
A shop has 20 sweets. They sell 13. How many sweets are left?
Anna reads 4 pages every day. How many pages does she read in 5 days?
There are 12 eggs. A fox eats 5. How many eggs are left?
Jake has 9 toy cars. His mum gives him 6 more. How many cars does he have?
A jar has 24 sweets. 4 children share them equally. How many each?
There are 5 fish tanks with 6 fish each. How many fish altogether?
Maya has 30 cards. She gives 12 to her brother. How many does she keep?
A train has 8 carriages with 10 seats each. How many seats in total?
Ben scored 45 points in a game. He scored 28 more. What is his total?
A garden has 56 flowers. 19 flowers are picked. How many are left?

multiplication.hint: Count how many fish in each group, then add the groups
multiplication.fact: 2 groups of 2 is the same as 2 × 2 = 4

multiplication.hint: You have 3 groups. How many apples are in each group?
Count the stars in one group, then count all groups
Each group has 2 flowers. Count all 5 groups
multiplication.fact: 5 × 2 = 10. The 2 times table always goes up by 2!

multiplication.hint: You have 2 groups. Count the cookies in each group
multiplication.fact: 2 × 5 = 10. This is the same as 5 × 2 = 10. Order doesn't matter!

multiplication.hint: There is only 1 group. How many butterflies are in it?
multiplication.fact: 1 × 8 = 8. Multiplying by 1 always gives the same number back!

multiplication.hint: Each group has just 1 star. How many groups are there?
multiplication.fact: 4 × 1 = 4. Multiplying by 1 never changes the number.

multiplication.hint: Count on from 0: +2, +2, +2. Where do you land?
Jump along a number line: +5, +5. Where do you land?
Count up four jumps of 2: 2, 4, 6, ...
Count the dots in one row, then count the rows
multiplication.fact: A 2 by 3 array has 2 rows with 3 in each row. 2 × 3 = 6

multiplication.hint: How many rows? How many dots in each row? Multiply!
Count one row at a time: 2, 4, 6
multiplication.fact: 3 rows of 2 = 6. Notice 3×2 and 2×3 both equal 6!

multiplication.hint: Think: how many groups of 2 make 4?
multiplication.fact: 2 × 2 = 4. We need 2 groups of 2 to make 4.

multiplication.hint: Count up in 2s until you reach 6: 2, 4, 6. How many jumps?
multiplication.fact: 2 × 3 = 6. Three jumps of 2 brings us to 6.

multiplication.hint: Think: how many groups of 5 make 10?

multiplication.story: There are {a} baskets. Each basket has {b} apples. How many apples altogether?
multiplication.hint: Draw 2 circles. Put 3 apples in each. Then count them all.

multiplication.story: There are {a} fish tanks. Each tank has {b} fish. How many fish altogether?
multiplication.hint: Think: 5 groups of 2. Count them: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10
Count the stars in one group, then count 3 groups
4 groups of 3 apples. Try counting: 3, 6, 9, 12
Count one group: 4 stars. Now count 4 groups.
multiplication.fact: 4 × 4 = 16. Four groups of 4 is like counting: 4, 8, 12, 16

multiplication.hint: 3 groups of 6. Try: 6 + 6 + 6 = ?
4 groups of 5. Try skip counting by 5: 5, 10, 15, 20
5 groups of 4. Tip: you can also think of it as 4 groups of 5!
multiplication.fact: 5 × 4 = 20. This is the same as 4 × 5 = 20. Switching order works!

multiplication.hint: Count one row at a time: 5, 10, 15
This is a square array! Count rows × columns.
multiplication.fact: 4 × 4 = 16. A square array means both sides are equal!

multiplication.hint: Count the dots in one row, then multiply by the number of rows
Switching the order of multiplication gives the same answer!
multiplication.fact: 3 × 4 = 4 × 3 = 12. This is called the commutative property!

multiplication.hint: Both arrays have the same total dots, just arranged differently
multiplication.fact: 4 × 5 = 5 × 4 = 20. You can always swap the numbers in multiplication!

multiplication.hint: Turn the array on its side — same dots, different arrangement
multiplication.fact: 6 × 3 = 3 × 6 = 18. Swapping the order never changes the answer!

multiplication.hint: Count up in 3s until you reach 12: 3, 6, 9, 12. How many jumps?
multiplication.fact: 3 × 4 = 12. It takes 4 jumps of 3 to reach 12.

multiplication.hint: Count up in 4s: 4, 8, 12, 16. How many jumps did you make?
How many groups of 6 make 18? Try: 6, 12, 18
Count by 5s until you reach 25: 5, 10, 15, 20, 25
multiplication.fact: 5 × 5 = 25. Five groups of 5 gives 25. This is 5 squared!

multiplication.story: There are {a} friends at a party. Each friend gets {b} balloons. How many balloons altogether?
multiplication.hint: Draw 4 groups of 3 balloons each. Then count them all.

multiplication.story: There are {a} vases on a table. Each vase has {b} flowers. How many flowers altogether?
multiplication.hint: Think: 3 groups of 6. Count by 6: 6, 12, 18

multiplication.story: There are {a} bags of sweets. Each bag has {b} sweets inside. How many sweets altogether?
multiplication.hint: 5 groups of 4. Count: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20
7 groups of 4. Try counting by 4: 4, 8, 12, 16, 20, 24, 28
9 groups of 3. Tip: try 10×3=30, then subtract 1 group of 3
8 groups of 4. Try: 8 × 4 = double of (4 × 4) = double 16
multiplication.fact: 8 × 4 = 32. Doubling trick: 4×4=16, double it to get 8×4=32

multiplication.hint: Try: 10×4=40, then subtract one group of 4
Count one row of 4, then count 8 rows. Or use the doubling trick!

multiplication.fact: 9 rows of 3 = 27. The 9s trick saves counting: 10×3−3=27

multiplication.hint: Count by 7: 7,14,21,28. How many jumps to reach 28?

multiplication.fact: 9 × 3 = 27. The 9s pattern: digits in answer always add up to 9! (2+7=9)

multiplication.hint: Count by 8: 8, 16, 24, 32. How many 8s?

multiplication.fact: 7 × 7 = 49. This is called 7 squared! 7×7 is a square number.
9 × 4 = 36. The digit trick: 3+6=9. The 9 times table always adds to 9!

multiplication.hint: The array stays the same size whether you rotate it or not!
multiplication.fact: 7 × 4 = 4 × 7 = 28. This always works, no matter how big the numbers!

multiplication.hint: Swap the numbers around — the total never changes!

multiplication.story: There are {a} shelves in a library. Each shelf holds {b} books. How many books altogether?
multiplication.hint: 7 groups of 4. Try: count by 4 seven times: 4,8,12,16,20,24,28

multiplication.story: There are {a} boxes of balls. Each box has {b} balls inside. How many balls altogether?
multiplication.hint: 9 groups of 3. Shortcut: 10×3=30, then take away 1 group of 3

multiplication.story: There are {a} children in art class. Each child gets {b} crayons. How many crayons altogether?
multiplication.hint: 8 groups of 5. Tip: count by 5 eight times, or try 8×5=half of 8×10!
